title: [InstCombine] Don't change fn signature for calls to declarations

tests.source_program:
target datalayout = "e-p:64:64"

declare i64 @external_func()

define ptr @caller() {
entry:
  %cast = bitcast i64 ()* @external_func to ptr ()*
  %result = call ptr %cast()
  ret ptr %result
}
tests.expect_optimized_program:
target datalayout = "e-p:64:64"

declare i64 @external_func()

define ptr @caller() {
entry:
  %cast = bitcast i64 ()* @external_func to ptr ()*
  %result = call ptr %cast()
  ret ptr %result
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary focuses on return type compatibility and vararg handling, but doesn't cover bitcast transformations between integer and pointer function types for declarations

alive2.src:
target datalayout = "e-p:64:64"

declare i64 @external_func()

define ptr @caller() {
entry:
  %cast = bitcast i64 ()* @external_func to ptr ()*
  %result = call ptr %cast()
  ret ptr %result
}
alive2.tgt:
; ModuleID = '<stdin>'
source_filename = "<stdin>"
target datalayout = "e-p:64:64"

declare i64 @external_func()

define ptr @caller() {
entry:
  %result = call ptr @external_func()
  ret ptr %result
}
alive2.log:
----------------------------------------
declare i64 @external_func()

@external_func = constant ? bytes, exec, align 8

define ptr @caller() {
entry:
  %cast = bitcast ptr @external_func to ptr
  %result = call ptr %cast()
  ret ptr %result
}
=>
declare i64 @external_func()

@external_func = constant ? bytes, exec, align 8

define ptr @caller() {
entry:
  %result = call ptr @external_func()
  ret ptr %result
}

****************************************
WARNING: Source function is always UB.
It can be refined by any target function.
Please make sure this is what you wanted.
****************************************

Transformation seems to be correct!

Summary:
  1 correct transformations
  0 incorrect transformations
  0 failed-to-prove transformations
  0 Alive2 errors

tests.source_program:
target datalayout = "e-p:64:64"

declare i32 @vararg_decl(i32, ...)

define i32 @caller() {
entry:
  %cast = bitcast i32 (i32, ...)* @vararg_decl to i32 (i32, i32)*
  %result = call i32 %cast(i32 1, i32 2)
  ret i32 %result
}
tests.expect_optimized_program:
target datalayout = "e-p:64:64"

declare i32 @vararg_decl(i32, ...)

define i32 @caller() {
entry:
  %cast = bitcast i32 (i32, ...)* @vararg_decl to i32 (i32, i32)*
  %result = call i32 %cast(i32 1, i32 2)
  ret i32 %result
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary discusses vararg handling simplification but doesn't cover cases where vararg declaration is cast to fixed-argument function type with mismatched parameter counts

tests.source_program:
target datalayout = "e-p:64:64"

declare coldcc i32 @cold_decl(i32)

define i32 @caller(i32 %x) {
entry:
  %cast = bitcast i32 (i32)* @cold_decl to i32 (i32)*
  %result = call coldcc i32 %cast(i32 %x)
  ret i32 %result
}
tests.expect_optimized_program:
target datalayout = "e-p:64:64"

declare coldcc i32 @cold_decl(i32)

define i32 @caller(i32 %x) {
entry:
  %cast = bitcast i32 (i32)* @cold_decl to i32 (i32)*
  %result = call coldcc i32 %cast(i32 %x)
  ret i32 %result
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary doesn't address calling convention preservation for declarations - coldcc and other non-default calling conventions create additional ABI constraints not covered in the generalization discussion
